title: Creamy Egyptian Chicken Tagine
description: A rich and creamy Egyptian dish featuring caramelized onions, dark brown sugar, and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a date-night dinner. This recipe is egg-free and offers a unique citrusy flavor profile.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are caramelized and golden brown, about 20-25 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the dark brown sugar, chicken broth, and lemon juice to the pot and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened, about 20-25 minutes.
Stir in the canned coconut milk and continue to cook, uncovered, until the sauce has thickened to your liking, about 5-10 minutes.
Serve the chicken and sauce over cooked rice or with crusty bread, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
